questions.question: What are the 'home row' keys for the left hand in touch typing?

options.option: A, S, D, F
options.comment: Left hand home row: A (pinky), S (ring), D (middle), F (index). Right hand: J, K, L, ; (semi-colon).

options.option: Q, W, E, R
Z, X, C, V

questions.question: What is the purpose of the small raised bumps on the F and J keys?

options.option: To help typists locate the home row position without looking at the keyboard
options.comment: Tactile bumps guide your index fingers to the home row keys F and J — foundation of touch typing.

options.option: To indicate that these keys have a different function than others
To separate the number row from the letter rows
They are a manufacturing defect with no intended purpose

questions.question: Which metric best measures typing performance?

options.option: WPM with accuracy — words per minute at a high accuracy rate
options.comment: WPM alone is misleading — 80 WPM at 70% accuracy produces more errors than 40 WPM at 99%.

options.option: Words per minute (WPM) only — the faster the better
How hard you press each key
The number of keys pressed per session regardless of errors

questions.question: What is 'touch typing'?

options.option: Typing without looking at the keyboard, using muscle memory
options.comment: Touch typists keep their eyes on the screen and source document — dramatically improving speed and accuracy.

options.option: Typing on a touchscreen device with two fingers
Typing very lightly to avoid damaging keyboard keys
Using a stylus instead of fingers to type on a tablet

questions.question: What is the correct posture for typing at a computer?

options.option: Feet flat on the floor, back straight, wrists level with the keyboard
options.comment: Good ergonomic posture prevents RSI and fatigue — essential for sustained data entry work.

options.option: Leaning forward with wrists resting on the keyboard constantly
Sitting as high as possible to see the keyboard better
Crossing your legs to raise your typing position

questions.question: Which finger is used to press the spacebar in standard touch typing technique?

options.option: Either thumb
options.comment: The spacebar is pressed with either thumb — the most common technique is to use the right thumb.

options.option: The right index finger
The left pinky finger
The middle finger of whichever hand is free

questions.question: In professional data entry, what always takes priority over speed?

options.option: Accuracy — errors in data cause downstream problems in business systems
options.comment: A fast but inaccurate typist creates more work than a slow accurate one. Fix accuracy first, then speed.

options.option: Speed — faster entry means more productivity
Formatting — consistent fonts matter most
Volume — the total number of records entered per day

questions.question: What is a 'source document' in data entry?

options.option: The original paper or file containing the information to be entered
options.comment: Always keep the source document available while entering data — it is the authority you verify against.

options.option: The spreadsheet template into which data is being entered
A government-issued form that must be submitted with every record
A style guide listing approved data entry formats

questions.question: How should dates be entered consistently in a dataset?

options.option: Using one agreed format throughout — e.g. DD/MM/YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD
options.comment: Mixing date formats (some 01/07/25, some July 1, 2025) corrupts sorting and filtering operations.

options.option: In whatever format is most natural to each individual data entry operator
Always writing the month as a word (e.g. January) to avoid confusion
Dates do not need a consistent format — systems detect them automatically

questions.question: What is the best way to verify an entry after you have typed it?

options.option: Compare it directly against the source document before moving on
options.comment: Verify as you go — catching an error immediately is faster than correcting a batch of errors later.

options.option: Print the entire dataset and check it at the end of the day
Ask a colleague to re-enter the same data and compare
Trust that spell check will catch any mistakes automatically

questions.question: Why is consistent capitalisation important in a database (e.g. always 'Mr' not 'mr' or 'MR')?

options.option: Inconsistent casing creates duplicate records and breaks searches and reports
options.comment: 'smith' and 'Smith' are treated as different values in searches, causing missed records and errors.

options.option: It makes the database look more professional when printed
Database systems require all-caps to store records correctly
It only matters for names, not for other types of data

questions.question: What should you do if you notice an error in someone else's data entry while reviewing a dataset?

options.option: Flag it to the supervisor and document it before correcting
options.comment: Changing data without authorisation can cause compliance issues. Always follow the correction process.

options.option: Silently fix it yourself without telling anyone
Delete the entire row to prevent incorrect data from spreading
Leave it — it is not your responsibility

questions.question: What is a document template?

options.option: A pre-designed file with placeholders for repeated use without modifying the original
options.comment: Templates (.dotx in Word) ensure consistent formatting every time a new document is created from them.

options.option: A blank document that has been saved for the first time
A completed form that is stored as a reference for new entries
A PDF file that cannot be edited or modified

questions.question: What does pressing Tab do when filling in a form with multiple fields?

options.option: Moves the cursor to the next field
options.comment: Tab navigates between form fields. Shift+Tab goes back. This is faster than reaching for the mouse.

options.option: Submits the form to the server
Indents the text within the current field
Duplicates the content of the current field into the next one

questions.question: When saving a completed form, what naming convention is most professional?

options.option: Include the date, client name, and document type — e.g. 2025-07-01_Smith_Invoice
options.comment: Date-first names sort chronologically. Including client and type makes files easy to find without opening them.

options.option: Save it as 'Document1' — the system assigns unique numbers
Use only the current date — e.g. 01072025
Use your own name as the filename for accountability

questions.question: What is an asterisk (*) beside a form field label typically used to indicate?

options.option: The field is required and must be filled in before submission
options.comment: * = mandatory field. Leaving it blank will usually prevent the form from submitting.

options.option: The field accepts multiple entries
The field content will be kept confidential
The field has a character limit

questions.question: Why should you use an existing template rather than creating a new document from scratch each time?

options.option: Templates save time and ensure every document has consistent formatting and branding
options.comment: Using templates prevents formatting drift and ensures all documents produced by a team look the same.

options.option: Templates are required by law for all business documents
Creating from scratch uses more printer ink
Templates automatically fill in all the required data

questions.question: When filling in a Word form protected with form fields, you can:

options.option: Type only in designated field areas — the rest of the document is locked
options.comment: Protected forms restrict editing to the form fields only, preventing accidental layout changes.

options.option: Edit any part of the document freely
Only use the keyboard, not the mouse, to fill the form
Right-click to unlock all fields before completing them

questions.question: What does Win + D do in Windows?

options.option: Shows the Desktop by minimising all open windows
options.comment: Win+D is a fast way to clear the screen and access desktop shortcuts or files.

options.option: Opens the Documents folder in File Explorer
Duplicates the current window
Opens the Default Programs settings

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to 'Redo' a previously undone action?

options.option: Ctrl + Y
options.comment: Ctrl+Z = Undo. Ctrl+Y = Redo (reverses an undo). Some apps also use Ctrl+Shift+Z.

options.option: Ctrl + Z
Ctrl + R
Ctrl + Shift + Z (all apps)

questions.question: What does Ctrl + Home do in Microsoft Excel?

options.option: Moves the cursor to cell A1 (beginning of the spreadsheet)
options.comment: Ctrl+Home = A1. Ctrl+End = last used cell. Essential for quick navigation in large spreadsheets.

options.option: Saves and closes the workbook
Selects the entire first row of the spreadsheet
Opens a new workbook

questions.question: What does Ctrl + F do in most Windows applications?

options.option: Opens a Find dialog to search for text within the document
options.comment: Ctrl+F (Find) works in Word, Excel, browsers, File Explorer, and most other apps.

options.option: Opens the File menu
Formats the selected text as bold
Creates a new folder in File Explorer

questions.question: What does pressing Ctrl + A do?

options.option: Selects all content in the current document, spreadsheet, or folder
options.comment: Ctrl+A selects everything — all text in Word, all cells in Excel, all files in a folder.

options.option: Opens a new application
Adds a new row below the current one
Aligns the selected text to the left

questions.question: What does Alt + F4 do in Windows?

options.option: Closes the currently active window or application
options.comment: Alt+F4 closes the active app. If no apps are open, it shows the Windows shutdown dialog.

options.option: Opens the function key settings in Windows
Takes a screenshot of the active window
Switches the monitor to full-screen mode

questions.question: What is 'Paste Special' used for in Microsoft Office?

options.option: Pasting only specific parts of copied content (e.g. values only, formatting only)
options.comment: Paste Special (Ctrl+Alt+V) is essential in Excel — paste values removes formulas, preventing errors.

options.option: Pasting encrypted content that requires a password
Pasting content into a locked or protected cell
Pasting content at a specific font size defined in the clipboard

questions.question: When copying text from a website into Word, the text often brings unwanted formatting. The best way to paste it cleanly is:

options.option: Use 'Keep Text Only' (or Ctrl+Shift+V) to paste without the website's formatting
options.comment: 'Keep Text Only' removes fonts, colours, and hyperlinks — giving you plain text you can format yourself.

options.option: Always paste using Ctrl+V — it removes formatting automatically
Copy the text into Notepad first, then copy again and paste into Word
Manually delete formatting by pressing Backspace after pasting

questions.question: When copying a formula in Excel that uses cell references, the references:

options.option: Adjust automatically to reflect the new cell location (relative references)
options.comment: Relative references (e.g. A1) update when you copy the formula. Absolute references ($A$1) stay fixed.

options.option: Always stay fixed regardless of where you paste
Are deleted and must be retyped manually
Convert to the values at the time of copying

questions.question: What does Ctrl + H open in most Office applications?

options.option: Find & Replace — lets you replace one text string with another throughout the document
options.comment: Ctrl+H opens Find & Replace. In Excel, use it to correct consistent errors across many cells at once.

options.option: The document History panel
A hidden field view showing tracked changes
The hyperlink insertion dialog

questions.question: To paste only the calculated values (not formulas) from Excel cells, use Paste Special and choose:

options.option: Values
options.comment: Paste Values converts formula results to static numbers. Useful before deleting the source data or sharing a file.

options.option: Formulas
Formats
All (same as regular Ctrl+V)

questions.question: You accidentally copied incorrect data and pasted it over 50 cells. What is the fastest fix?

options.option: Press Ctrl + Z immediately to undo the paste
options.comment: Ctrl+Z undoes the last action. Multiple presses undo further back. Always undo before changing anything else.

options.option: Manually retype all 50 cells with the correct data
Close the file without saving and reopen it
Use Find & Replace to search for the incorrect value

questions.question: What does 'Freeze Panes' do in Excel?

options.option: Locks row(s) or column(s) so they stay visible when scrolling
options.comment: Freeze the top row to keep column headers visible as you scroll down through thousands of records.

options.option: Prevents any cells in the sheet from being edited
Saves the current scroll position for next time you open the file
Combines multiple rows into a single frozen summary row

questions.question: What is the benefit of converting a data range into an Excel 'Table' (Ctrl+T)?

options.option: Tables auto-expand, include built-in filters, and extend formulas to new rows automatically
options.comment: Tables (Insert > Table) are the best practice for structured data — they make management much easier.

options.option: Tables prevent anyone from adding new rows or columns
Tables convert all formulas to static values automatically
Tables link the data to an external database

questions.question: To quickly find the last row of data in a large Excel dataset, press:

options.option: Ctrl + End
options.comment: Ctrl+End jumps to the last used cell in the sheet. Ctrl+Down Arrow jumps to the last filled cell in a column.

options.option: Ctrl + Home
Ctrl + L
Ctrl + Shift + End

questions.question: What does 'Split View' in Excel allow you to do?

options.option: View two different parts of a large spreadsheet simultaneously in the same window
options.comment: Split View (View > Split) divides the window so you can scroll sections independently.

options.option: Open the same spreadsheet in two separate Excel windows
Display two worksheets side by side from the same workbook
Separate the sheet into odd and even row views

questions.question: When entering data into a large Excel range, pressing Enter after each entry moves the cursor:

options.option: Down to the next row in the same column
options.comment: Enter moves down. Tab moves right. For column-by-column entry, use Tab; for row-by-row, use Enter.

options.option: Right to the next cell in the same row
To the next empty cell in the spreadsheet in any direction
It saves the data and stays in the same cell

questions.question: What does Ctrl + Shift + End do in Excel?

options.option: Extends the selection from the current cell to the last used cell in the sheet
options.comment: Ctrl+Shift+End selects a range from your current cell down to the last used cell — quick for selecting all data.

options.option: Deletes everything from the current cell to the end of the sheet
Moves the cursor to the last cell of the current row
Freezes everything below the current row

questions.question: What does the Excel TRIM() function do?

options.option: Removes extra spaces from text, leaving only single spaces between words
options.comment: TRIM() fixes the most common data cleaning issue — extra spaces copied from web pages or other systems.

options.option: Shortens text to a specified number of characters
Removes all spaces from a text string entirely
Trims a number to the specified number of decimal places

questions.question: Which Excel function converts text to all uppercase letters?

options.option: UPPER()
options.comment: UPPER() converts to capitals. LOWER() converts to lowercase. PROPER() capitalises the first letter of each word.

options.option: CAPS()
TEXT()
CONVERT()

questions.question: How do you remove duplicate rows from an Excel dataset?

options.option: Data tab > Remove Duplicates, then select which columns to check
options.comment: Remove Duplicates (Data tab) identifies and deletes duplicate rows based on the columns you specify.

options.option: Sort the data and manually delete duplicate rows
Use Conditional Formatting to colour duplicates, then delete them
Apply a filter to hide duplicates without deleting them

questions.question: What is the PROPER() function used for in Excel?

options.option: Capitalises the first letter of each word in a text string
options.comment: =PROPER('john smith') returns 'John Smith'. Useful for standardising inconsistently cased name fields.

options.option: Checks a cell for common spelling errors
Validates that text matches a predefined pattern
Converts text to proper sentence case (first letter only)

questions.question: What is a 'double-entry check' in data entry?

options.option: Entering critical data twice and comparing both inputs to catch errors
options.comment: Double-entry is used for high-value or critical data (e.g. bank account numbers) where errors are costly.

options.option: Entering every record twice to ensure it is saved correctly
Filling in a second backup copy of every form
Using two different computers to enter the same data simultaneously

questions.question: What does using Find & Replace (Ctrl+H) help you fix in a dataset?

options.option: A systematic error repeated throughout the file (e.g. wrong abbreviation or spelling)
options.comment: If 'UK' was entered as 'U.K.' everywhere, one Replace All fixes the entire column instantly.

options.option: Formula errors like #N/A and #VALUE!
Cells that contain numbers formatted as text
Missing entries in a required field

questions.question: Which approach helps you handle multiple urgent tasks when everything seems equally important?

options.option: Prioritise by deadline and impact, then tackle the highest-priority task first
options.comment: When everything is 'urgent', sort by deadline (when it is due) and impact (what happens if it is late).

options.option: Start the easiest task first to warm up for the harder ones
Work on all tasks simultaneously by switching between them every few minutes
Ask your manager to do the most difficult task for you

questions.question: What is the 'Urgent vs Important' matrix used for?

options.option: Categorising tasks so you tackle high-impact work before reactive fire-fighting
options.comment: Important but not urgent tasks (planning, learning) are often neglected — the matrix ensures they are not.

options.option: Measuring how long urgent tasks take compared to important ones
Deciding which tasks to permanently delete from your to-do list
A performance appraisal tool used by managers only

questions.question: If you realise you cannot meet a deadline, the professional action is to:

options.option: Inform the requester as early as possible and propose a revised timeline
options.comment: Early communication allows the requester to adjust plans. Saying nothing at the last moment causes more harm.

options.option: Stay silent and submit incomplete work on time
Submit the work late without telling anyone
Ask a colleague to submit on your behalf so the deadline is technically met

questions.question: What is the best use of a digital calendar in a data entry role?

options.option: Block time for recurring tasks, set reminders for deadlines, and avoid double-booking
options.comment: A well-managed calendar prevents missed deadlines and helps you protect time for focused data entry work.

options.option: Record the exact time taken to complete every individual keystroke
Automatically generate daily reports of your productivity
Monitor other team members' work schedules

questions.question: What is 'time blocking'?

options.option: Scheduling specific time slots for specific tasks to avoid task-switching
options.comment: Time blocking reduces context-switching overhead — ideal for focused data entry that requires concentration.

options.option: A security feature that blocks apps after a set number of hours
Blocking time in a colleague's calendar without their permission
A feature in Outlook that prevents meeting invites from being sent

questions.question: A daily work checklist primarily helps you:

options.option: Track what needs to be done, ensure nothing is missed, and review progress
options.comment: Checklists externalise memory, freeing mental capacity for the actual work.

options.option: Automatically submit your completed tasks to your manager
Measure your typing speed at the start of each day
Generate an invoice for the hours you worked

questions.question: What is Excel's 'Flash Fill' feature?

options.option: Automatically fills a column based on a pattern it detects from the first entry
options.comment: Flash Fill (Ctrl+E) detects patterns like combining first+last name or extracting a domain from an email.

options.option: Quickly fills all empty cells with the value from the cell above
Copies the entire column to a new worksheet in one step
A shortcut to apply the last used format to selected cells

questions.question: What is a Macro in Microsoft Excel?

options.option: A recorded series of actions that can be replayed with a single button press
options.comment: Macros are recorded via Developer > Record Macro and save time on repetitive multi-step tasks.

options.option: A large spreadsheet formula that performs many calculations at once
A type of chart used to display large datasets
A keyboard shortcut for formatting an entire row

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut to trigger Flash Fill in Excel is:

options.option: Ctrl + E
options.comment: Ctrl+E invokes Flash Fill. Alternatively, it activates automatically when Excel detects a pattern.

options.option: Ctrl + F
Ctrl + Shift + F
Alt + E

questions.question: When recording a Macro, what should you do to ensure it runs correctly?

options.option: Plan and test every step before recording to avoid mistakes in the macro
options.comment: Every action during recording is captured — a wrong step creates a wrong macro. Plan first.

options.option: Type as fast as possible so the macro runs faster
Keep all data on one sheet so the macro never gets confused
Record while other applications are open to test real-world speed

questions.question: Which Excel feature automatically fills a series (e.g. Jan, Feb, Mar) when you drag the fill handle?

options.option: AutoFill
options.comment: AutoFill recognises sequences: months, days, numbers, and dates. Drag the small square handle to extend the pattern.

options.option: AutoSum
Flash Fill
AutoFormat

questions.question: What file format must you save an Excel workbook in to retain Macro functionality?

options.option: .xlsm (Excel Macro-Enabled Workbook)
options.comment: Saving a macro file as .xlsx will strip the macros. Always save as .xlsm to preserve them.

options.option: .xlsx (standard Excel format)
.xls (legacy Excel format)
.csv (comma-separated values)

questions.question: What is a professional file naming convention for a document saved on 3 July 2025?

options.option: 2025-07-03_ClientName_Invoice.docx (date-first, YYYY-MM-DD)
options.comment: YYYY-MM-DD sorts files chronologically in any file manager. Descriptive names make content clear at a glance.

options.option: Invoice_ClientName_3rd July 2025.docx
NewDocument(final)(v3)LATEST.docx
03-07-25 invoice.docx

questions.question: What is the purpose of having a logical folder hierarchy?

options.option: To find any file quickly without needing to use search every time
options.comment: A well-planned folder structure (Year > Department > Project) reduces time wasted searching for files.

options.option: To ensure Windows does not run out of storage space
To back up files automatically to the cloud
To prevent other users from accessing your files

questions.question: What does 'versioning' a document mean (e.g. Report_v1, Report_v2)?

options.option: Saving copies at key stages so you can go back to an earlier version if needed
options.comment: Versioning is critical for documents that are edited by multiple people over time.

options.option: Adding a version number only to the first draft of every document
Using a different file format for each version (v1=.doc, v2=.docx)
Sending each version to a different recipient for review

questions.question: What is document archiving?

options.option: Moving completed or old documents to a separate storage area for future reference
options.comment: Archiving keeps working folders clean without permanently deleting records that may be needed later.

options.option: Permanently deleting documents that are no longer in use
Converting documents to PDF before they are printed
Encrypting confidential documents before sending by email

questions.question: Why should spaces in file names sometimes be avoided when sharing files digitally?

options.option: Some systems or URLs encode spaces as %20 which can cause link or path errors
options.comment: Use hyphens (-) or underscores (_) instead of spaces for safe cross-platform file names.

options.option: File names with spaces are automatically flagged as spam by email servers
Windows does not allow spaces in file names
Files with spaces take more storage space than files without

questions.question: What does a document retention policy define?

options.option: How long different types of documents must be kept and when they can be destroyed
options.comment: Retention policies are often legally required — financial records, HR files, and contracts have minimum periods.

options.option: The maximum file size allowed for documents stored on the company network
The approved fonts and page sizes for all company documents
Who is allowed to create new folders in the shared drive
